title: Resume bullet — AEM delivery impact
body: Delivered [feature/migration you actually worked on] on AEM as a Cloud Service for [brand/program], improving [metric you can explain, e.g. publish lead time / cache hit ratio / Core Web Vitals] by [your measured change] through [Dispatcher tuning / pipeline automation / component refactor you did].

title: Resume bullet — headless / GraphQL
body: Implemented headless content APIs using AEM Content Fragments and persisted GraphQL queries, enabling [web/mobile] teams to [specific outcome you delivered] without duplicate content entry.

title: STAR story frame — production incident
body: Situation: [outage/symptom]. Task: [your role]. Action: [triage steps, collaboration, fix]. Result: [restored SLA, post-mortem, preventive change].

title: LinkedIn summary pattern — AEM developer
body: AEM Developer with hands-on experience in [Sites/Assets/Cloud Service] and [Dispatcher/headless/personalization]. [One sentence on a real project outcome you can explain — e.g. reliable releases, faster publish cycle, or integration you owned.] Background in [Java/frontend/platform] and [industry]. Open to AEM roles in [city/remote].

title: Recruiter outreach — AEM roles
body: Hi [Name], this is [Your name]. I am an AEM [level] developer with experience in [Cloud Service / Dispatcher / headless]. I am exploring [company/sector] roles where Adobe Experience Manager is central to the stack. Open to a brief conversation if this aligns with roles you are hiring for in [team/location].

title: Naukri profile headline & summary — AEM
body:
Headline: AEM Developer | [Sites / AEMaaCS / Dispatcher] | [City / Remote]

Summary: AEM developer with [N] years on Adobe Experience Manager ([Sites/Assets/Cloud Service]). [1–2 outcomes from your real work that you can explain in an interview.] Comfortable with Java, Sling/HTL, and [Dispatcher / headless / integrations you have used]. Open to AEM roles in [locations]. Prefer roles where AEM is core to the product, not a side CMS.

title: Resume bullet — Dispatcher / caching
body: Improved publish-side performance for [site/program you worked on] by tuning Dispatcher [filter/cache/invalidation] rules and measuring [cache hit ratio / TTFB / error rate you tracked], reducing [symptom you observed] without changing authoring workflows.
